Title: Lifestyle effects
ChapterText:
Managing social anxiety is not purely a psychological endeavor. People suffering social anxiety often lead unhealthy lifestyle. They sleep irregularly, especially during time of heightened stress and anxiety. This can lead irritability and can heighten anxiety.

Many choose to escape negative thoughts and emotions by spending a lot of time online. Gaming, scrolling posts on social medias or watching movies are their bread and butter.
These activities are not by any means wrong.
 But spending hours and hours online and isolating oneself can have negative effect on mental health and overall wellbeing.

Poor diet can lead to higher risk of depression, anxiety or cognitive decline.

Title: Sleep
ChapterText:
It has been stated many times how crucial sleep is. Still many of us go to bed late, long after midnight and hope there will be no consequences. But usually we wake up drained, in bad mood and even slightly angry we did it again. It is proved that long period of bad sleep leads to anxiety and depression.
Thus making life with social anxiety even harder.

Also when you don't have energy you cannot face social anxiety.

Title: Replace the bad with the good
ChapterText:
Aim for 7-9 hours of consistent sleep.

Limit caffeine, especially before social events. Caffeine mimics the physical symptoms of anxiety (racing heart, jitters), which can trick your brain into feeling a panic attack. 

Also start doing some excercises or sport. You don't need a gym. A brisk walk or a short yoga flow helps burn off cortisol (the stress hormone). Movement teaches your brain that a fast heartbeat is safe, making you more resilient when you feel nervous in public.

Title: Be kind to yourself
ChapterText:
When applying change to your life, even the positive one, you may slip back to your old state sometimes, or even stop with the change completely. You might feel dissapointed or even angry at yourself afterwards, but do not beat yourself. Do not let your inner critic win. It takes time to rewire your behaviour, we are creatures of habits, it is easy for us to do thing we do often and for a long time. No one becomes socially anxious in a week or month.
It took you years to shape you to your current state of mind and of life.

Title: Final words
ChapterText:
Be proud of yourself, that you decided to fight back the social anxiety and decided to claim your life back.
It takes some courage. Life with social anxiety can be very draining, can make you feel that there is no way out.
But it is totally possible to get free from social anxiety.

All the negative programming from the past you might be internally  can be with steady pace changed. Old wounds can be healed and  new, deep connections with others can be established.

So let your true self shine and become not only a source of respect for yourself, but also a source of inspiration for others. Believe in yourself. You are on a good path.
